title: 조회수 백만 가자!
scene: 🪵 할머니댁 대청마루 · Grandma's House — Wooden Porch

goal.ko: 추측·평가·강조 표현으로 상황을 생생하게 말할 수 있어요!
goal.en: I can vividly describe situations using inference, evaluation, and emphasis!

key_points: -는 모양이다 — inferring from what you observe
-(으)ㄹ 만하다 — something is worth doing / decent
-는 데다가 — not only X but also Y

script.speaker: 미래
script.text: 자, 오늘 드디어 댄스 챌린지 찍는 날이야!
script.en: Okay, today is finally the day we film the dance challenge!

script.speaker: 올리비아
script.en: I was studying for exams so I couldn't even practice.

script.speaker: 미래
script.text: 그동안 유튜브 많이 봤으니 외울 만하잖아?
script.en: You've been watching YouTube a lot, so it should be worth memorizing, right?

script.speaker: 올리비아
script.text: 보는 거랑 직접 하는 거는 다르지.
script.en: Watching and actually doing it are different.

script.speaker: 리아
script.text: 올리비아 언니, 표정을 보니까 엄청 긴장한 모양이에요.
script.en: Olivia unnie, looking at your expression it seems like you're super nervous.

script.speaker: 올리비아
script.text: 당연하지! 카메라 앞에서 춤을 추는 건데.
script.en: Of course! I'm dancing in front of a camera.

script.speaker: 올리비아
script.text: 비트도 빠른 데다가 동작도 너무 커.
script.en: Not only is the beat fast, but the moves are too big.

script.speaker: 미래
script.text: 악! 나 동작을 놓쳤어. 컷! 다시!
script.en: Argh! I missed a move. Cut! Again!

script.speaker: 리아
script.en: Unnie, let's watch it back once.

script.speaker: 미래
script.en: This is actually worth watching more than I thought!

script.speaker: 올리비아
script.text: 우리 잘한다~ 한번 더 해 보자!
script.en: We're good! Let's do it one more time!

script.speaker: 리아
script.en: Let's go for a million views! Yay!

grammar.title: -는 모양이다
grammar.explanation_en: Used when you observe evidence and INFER something — like a detective reading the signs. Much more vivid than just '것 같다'.
grammar.pattern: Verb -는 모양이다 / Adj -(으)ㄴ 모양이다 / Noun인 모양이다

scene_example.en: Looking at your expression, it seems like you're really nervous.

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 Action verb → -는 모양이다
rule_boxes.content:
자는 모양이야 = seems like she's sleeping
공부하는 모양이야 = seems like he's studying
울고 있는 모양이야 = seems like she's crying

rule_boxes.label: 🟢 Descriptive verb → -(으)ㄴ 모양이다
rule_boxes.content:
긴장한 모양이야 = seems like (she's) nervous
피곤한 모양이야 = seems like (he's) tired
바쁜 모양이야 = seems like (she's) busy

rule_boxes.label: 🟡 Past → -았/었나 봐 (더 자연스러운 회화체)
rule_boxes.content:
늦잠 잔 모양이야 ↔ 늦잠 잤나 봐
모양이다 = 관찰 직후 즉각 추론 (more immediate)
나 봐 = 사실 알고 나서 (after learning the fact)

examples.en: Looking at your expression, it seems like you're really nervous.
It seems like she didn't bring her phone.

examples.ko: 미래가 아직 안 온 걸 보니 늦는 모양이에요.
examples.en: Since Mirae hasn't come yet, it looks like she's running late.

grammar.title: -(으)ㄹ 만하다
grammar.explanation_en: Used to say something is 'worth doing' or 'decent enough' — not amazing, but genuinely recommendable.

scene_example.en: This is actually worth watching more than I thought!

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 Vowel-ending stem → -ㄹ 만하다
rule_boxes.content:
볼 만하다 = worth watching  (보다 → 볼)
살 만하다 = decent enough to live (살다 → 살)
먹을 만하다 = worth eating  (먹다 → 먹을)

rule_boxes.label: 🟢 In conversation
rule_boxes.content:
이 드라마 볼 만해! = This drama is worth watching!
이 식당 먹을 만해요. = This restaurant is decent.
외울 만하잖아? = It should be worth memorizing, right?

rule_boxes.label: 🟡 Nuance — not a rave review
rule_boxes.content:
최고야! ≠ 볼 만해
볼 만해 = it's watchable, decent, not bad
Tip: great for honest, balanced recommendations!

examples.en: This is actually worth watching more than I thought!

examples.ko: 그동안 유튜브 많이 봤으니 외울 만하잖아?
examples.en: You've watched YouTube a lot, so it should be worth memorizing, right?

examples.ko: 이 영화 볼 만해요. 한번 보세요!
examples.en: This movie is worth watching. Give it a try!

grammar.title: 조차
grammar.explanation_en: Attaches to a noun to mean 'even X' with a sense of surprise or disappointment — even the most basic thing didn't happen.

scene_example.en: I was studying for exams so I couldn't even practice.

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 N + 조차 = even N (surprised)
rule_boxes.content:
연습조차 못 했어 = couldn't even practice
이름조차 몰라 = don't even know the name
물조차 못 마셨어 = couldn't even drink water

rule_boxes.label: 🟡 조차 vs 도 vs 까지
rule_boxes.content:
나도 = me too (neutral)
나까지 = even me (adding one more)
나조차 = even me (shocked — I, of all people!)

rule_boxes.label: 🔴 Almost always negative context
rule_boxes.content:
✅ 연습조차 못 했어 (negative verb → OK)
❌ 연습조차 잘 했어 (positive → unnatural)
Tip: 조차 + 못/안/없다 = the combo to remember

examples.en: I was studying for exams so I couldn't even practice.
I was so busy I couldn't even eat.
I didn't even know the title of that movie.

grammar.title: -는 데다가
grammar.title_en: Not only X, but also Y
grammar.explanation_en: Stacks two qualities together — 'on top of X, also Y.' Makes descriptions richer and more vivid. Often used to complain OR to rave!

scene_example.ko: 비트도 빠른 데다가 동작도 너무 커.
scene_example.en: Not only is the beat fast, but the moves are too big.

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 Descriptive verb → -(으)ㄴ 데다가
rule_boxes.content:
비트가 빠른 데다가 동작도 커.
= Not only is the beat fast, but the moves are big.
맛있는 데다가 싸요. = Not only tasty but cheap.

rule_boxes.label: 🟢 Action verb → -는 데다가
rule_boxes.content:
노래도 잘하는 데다가 춤도 잘 춰!
= Not only does she sing well, she dances well too!
공부도 하는 데다가 아르바이트도 해요.

rule_boxes.label: 🟡 Works for complaints AND compliments
rule_boxes.content:
😤 COMPLAINT: 비싼 데다가 맛도 없어. (Expensive AND tasteless)
😍 COMPLIMENT: 재밌는 데다가 배울 것도 많아! (Fun AND educational)

examples.ko: 비트도 빠른 데다가 동작도 너무 커.
examples.en: Not only is the beat fast, but the moves are too big.

examples.ko: 한국어도 재밌는 데다가 배울 것도 많아요!
examples.en: Korean is not only fun but also has a lot to learn!
Not only is there a lot of homework, but there's also a test.

banmal_jondaemal.intro_ko: 이 에피소드에서 미래와 올리비아(친구)는 서로 반말, 리아는 친언니 미래에게는 반말, 언니 친구 올리비아에게는 존댓말을 써요.
banmal_jondaemal.intro_en: In this episode, Mirae and Olivia (friends) use casual speech with each other; Lia uses casual speech with her older sister Mirae, but polite speech with Olivia (Mirae's friend).

rows.english: Not only is the beat fast, but the moves are big.

vocab_comparison.intro_ko: 댄스 챌린지 영상을 같이 볼 때 쓸 수 있는 표현들이에요.
vocab_comparison.intro_en: Expressions you can use when watching the challenge video together.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: 요즘 젊은 애들 숏폼 영상 많이 찍는 모양이에요.
lines.en: It seems like young people are filming a lot of short-form videos these days.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: 맞아요. 시간도 걸리는 데다가 장비도 필요하던데요.
lines.en: Right. Not only does it take time, but you need equipment too.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: 한 번 봤는데 꽤 볼 만하더라고요!
lines.en: I watched one and it was actually quite worth watching!

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: They say it even exceeded a million views.

items.note: 앞 명사에 붙여서 발음: 연습조차 → [연습조차] (끊지 않음)

messages.text: 근데 나 동작 놓친 거 눈에 띄어ㅠㅠ

real_life.title: 📖 읽기 — 숏폼 영상의 인기
real_life.passage: 요즘 숏폼 영상이 정말 인기예요. 짧은 데다가 재미있어서 많은 사람들이 봐요. 만드는 것도 어렵지 않아요. 핸드폰조차 있으면 누구든지 만들 수 있거든요. 춤, 요리, 언어 공부 등 주제도 다양해요. 조회수가 높은 영상을 보면 내용이 볼 만한 것들이 많아요.

quiz.question: 빈칸에 알맞은 말은? '비트도 빠른 ___ 동작도 너무 커.'

self_check: 관찰하고 추측할 때 '-는 모양이다'를 쓸 수 있어요.
'볼 만하다', '먹을 만하다'처럼 추천할 때 '-ㄹ 만하다'를 쓸 수 있어요.
'연습조차 못 했어'처럼 강조할 때 '조차'를 쓸 수 있어요.
'빠른 데다가 커'처럼 두 특징을 연결할 때 '-는 데다가'를 쓸 수 있어요.
챌린지, 조회수, 동작, 비트 등 SNS·댄스 어휘를 알아요.

usage.heading: '-는 모양이다' vs '-것 같다'
usage.content: Both express guessing, but 모양이다 is based on observable evidence, while 것 같다 is a more general feeling.

examples.en: Looking at the expression, it seems like things are tough. (from evidence)

usage.heading: '조차' vs '도'
usage.content: '도' means 'also/even' neutrally. '조차' means 'even' with a negative, surprising nuance — 'not even X'.

examples.en: I couldn't even eat rice. (extreme emphasis — even the most basic thing)

usage.heading: '-는 데다가' — stacking qualities
usage.content: Use this to say something has quality A, AND ALSO quality B — both adding up.

examples.ko: 비트도 빠른 데다가 동작도 너무 커.
examples.en: Not only is the beat fast, but the moves are too big.

examples.ko: 한국어도 재밌는 데다가 배울 것도 많아요!
examples.en: Korean is not only fun but also has a lot to learn!
